([/vɪdoʊ/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"); born 8 February 1990) is an [Australian](/wiki/Australia "Australia")-born [professional wrestler](/wiki/Professional_wrestler "Professional wrestler") and [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") footballer of [Samoan](/wiki/Samoans "Samoans") descent. He is predominantly performing for [All Japan Pro Wrestling](/wiki/All_Japan_Pro_Wrestling "All Japan Pro Wrestling") under the [ring name](/wiki/Ring_name "Ring name") **Xyon**, where he is a former [World Tag Team Champion](/wiki/World_Tag_Team_Championship_(AJPW) "World Tag Team Championship (AJPW)") alongside [Oddyssey](/wiki/Odyssey_Jones "Odyssey Jones") as part of the HAVOC [stable](/wiki/Stable_(wrestling) "Stable (wrestling)"). He is best known for his tenure in [WWE](/wiki/WWE "WWE"), where he performed under the ring name **Xyon Quinn**. Born in Australia, he played for the [Samoa national rugby league team](/wiki/Samoa_national_rugby_league_team "Samoa national rugby league team").

**Daniel Sean Vidot** ([/vɪdoʊ/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"); born 8 February 1990) is an [Australian](/wiki/Australia "Australia")-born [professional wrestler](/wiki/Professional_wrestler "Professional wrestler") and [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") footballer of [Samoan](/wiki/Samoans "Samoans") descent. He is predominantly performing for [All Japan Pro Wrestling](/wiki/All_Japan_Pro_Wrestling "All Japan Pro Wrestling") under the [ring name](/wiki/Ring_name "Ring name") **Xyon**, where he is a former [World Tag Team Champion](/wiki/World_Tag_Team_Championship_(AJPW) "World Tag Team Championship (AJPW)") alongside [Oddyssey](/wiki/Odyssey_Jones "Odyssey Jones") as part of the HAVOC [stable](/wiki/Stable_(wrestling) "Stable (wrestling)"). He is best known for his tenure in [WWE](/wiki/WWE "WWE"), where he performed under the ring name **Xyon Quinn**. Born in Australia, he played for the [Samoa national rugby league team](/wiki/Samoa_national_rugby_league_team "Samoa national rugby league team").

During his time in rugby league, he previously played as a [winger](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#Wing "Rugby league positions") in the [NRL](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") for the [Canberra Raiders](/wiki/Canberra_Raiders "Canberra Raiders"), [St. George Illawarra Dragons](/wiki/St._George_Illawarra_Dragons "St. George Illawarra Dragons"), [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") and the [Gold Coast Titans](/wiki/Gold_Coast_Titans "Gold Coast Titans"). He also played for the [Salford Red Devils](/wiki/Salford_Red_Devils "Salford Red Devils") in the [Super League](/wiki/Super_League "Super League"), and represented Samoa and now the USA at international level, where he often plays as a [second-row](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#Second-row_forward "Rugby league positions").

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daniel_Vidot&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Born in [Brisbane](/wiki/Brisbane "Brisbane"), [Queensland](/wiki/Queensland "Queensland"), Vidot is of [Samoan](/wiki/Samoans "Samoans") and [Irish](/wiki/Irish_Australian "Irish Australian") descent.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Vidot played his junior football at [Souths](/wiki/Souths_Magpies "Souths Magpies") [Acacia Ridge](/wiki/Acacia_Ridge "Acacia Ridge"), and attended [Sunnybank State High School](/wiki/Sunnybank_State_High_School "Sunnybank State High School") and [St Thomas More College, Sunnybank](/wiki/St_Thomas_More_College,_Sunnybank "St Thomas More College, Sunnybank") before being signed the [Canberra Raiders](/wiki/Canberra_Raiders "Canberra Raiders"). In 2007, Vidot played the Queensland Under 17's team.[[6]](#cite_note-6) Vidot played for the [Canberra Raiders](/wiki/Canberra_Raiders "Canberra Raiders") [NYC](/wiki/National_Youth_Competition_(rugby_league) "National Youth Competition (rugby league)") team in 2008–2009 including playing in the Raiders team at [centre](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#Centre "Rugby league positions") that won the 2008 inaugural [Toyota Cup](/wiki/National_Youth_Competition_(rugby_league) "National Youth Competition (rugby league)") Grand Final against the [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") [NYC](/wiki/National_Youth_Competition_(rugby_league) "National Youth Competition (rugby league)") team 28–24.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Rugby league career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daniel_Vidot&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Rugby league career")]

### 2009

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daniel_Vidot&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 2009")]

In Round 18 of the [2009 NRL season](/wiki/2009_NRL_season "2009 NRL season"), Vidot was called on to make his first grade [NRL](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") debut for the [Canberra Raiders](/wiki/Canberra_Raiders "Canberra Raiders") against the [Newcastle Knights](/wiki/Newcastle_Knights "Newcastle Knights") on the [wing](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#Wing "Rugby league positions") in the Raiders 23–4 loss at [Hunter Stadium](/wiki/Hunter_Stadium "Hunter Stadium").[[8]](#cite_note-8) In Round 18 against the [Penrith Panthers](/wiki/Penrith_Panthers "Penrith Panthers"), Vidot scored his first NRL career try in the Raiders 27–14 loss at [Penrith Stadium](/wiki/Penrith_Stadium "Penrith Stadium").[[9]](#cite_note-9) In Round 25 against the [Newcastle Knights](/wiki/Newcastle_Knights "Newcastle Knights") at [Canberra Stadium](/wiki/Canberra_Stadium "Canberra Stadium"), Vidot was sin binned during the match after being in a fight with the Knight's [Ben Rogers](/wiki/Ben_Rogers_(rugby_league) "Ben Rogers (rugby league)"), the Raiders won the game 30–14 and Vidot scored a try.[[10]](#cite_note-10) Vidot finished his debut year in the NRL with him playing in 8 matches and scoring 6 tries for the Raiders in the 2009 NRL season.

### 2010

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daniel_Vidot&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2010")]

Vidot finished the [2010 NRL season](/wiki/2010_NRL_season "2010 NRL season") as the Raiders highest try scorer with 16 tries in 25 matches. Vidot was selected in the [Samoan](/wiki/Samoa_national_rugby_league_team "Samoa national rugby league team") train on squad in 2010 to play New Zealand but withdrew so he would remain eligible to play for [Queensland](/wiki/Queensland_rugby_league_team "Queensland rugby league team").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

### 2011

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daniel_Vidot&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2011")]

On 30 August 2011, Vidot signed with the St George Illawarra Dragons starting in the 2012 season on a 3-year deal after falling out of favour with the Raiders spending most of the year in the [Queensland Cup](/wiki/Queensland_Cup "Queensland Cup") playing for the [Souths Logan Magpies](/wiki/Souths_Logan_Magpies "Souths Logan Magpies").[[12]](#cite_note-12) Vidot finished the [2011 NRL season](/wiki/2011_NRL_season "2011 NRL season") with him playing in 12 matches and scoring 5 tries for the Raiders.

### 2012

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daniel_Vidot&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2012")]

In Round 3 of the [2012 NRL season](/wiki/2012_NRL_season "2012 NRL season"), Vidot made his club debut for the [St George Illawarra Dragons](/wiki/St_George_Illawarra_Dragons "St George Illawarra Dragons") against the [Wests Tigers](/wiki/Wests_Tigers "Wests Tigers") on the wing, scoring a try in the Dragons 36–12 win at [Jubilee Oval](/wiki/Jubilee_Oval "Jubilee Oval").[[13]](#cite_note-13) Vidot finished the year with him playing in 20 matches and scoring 5 tries for the Dragons.

### 2013

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daniel_Vidot&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: 2013")]

On 20 April 2013, Vidot played in the [2013 Polynesian Cup](/wiki/2013_Polynesian_Cup "2013 Polynesian Cup") against [Tonga](/wiki/Tonga_national_rugby_league_team "Tonga national rugby league team"), scoring the Samoan's only try in the 36–4 defeat at Penrith Stadium.[[14]](#cite_note-14)
Vidot played in 15 matches scored 9 tries for the Dragons in the [2013 NRL season](/wiki/2013_NRL_season "2013 NRL season"). On 15 October, Daniel Vidot signed a 2-year deal with the Brisbane Broncos.[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16) Vidot appeared in all four of Samoa's [World Cup](/wiki/2013_Rugby_League_World_Cup "2013 Rugby League World Cup") games and scored one try against [France](/wiki/France_national_rugby_league_team "France national rugby league team") in their 22–6 win at [Stade Gilbert Brutus](/wiki/Stade_Gilbert_Brutus "Stade Gilbert Brutus").[[17]](#cite_note-17)

### 2014

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daniel_Vidot&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: 2014")]

In Round 1 of the [season](/wiki/2014_NRL_season "2014 NRL season"), Vidot made his club debut for the [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") against the [Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs](/wiki/Canterbury-Bankstown_Bulldogs "Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs") on the wing, scoring a try in the Broncos 18–12 win at [ANZ Stadium](/wiki/ANZ_Stadium "ANZ Stadium").[[18]](#cite_note-18) On 3 May 2014, Vidot was selected to play for Samoa in the [2014 Pacific Test](/wiki/2014_Rugby_League_Four_Nations#Samoa_v._Fiji "2014 Rugby League Four Nations") against [Fiji](/wiki/Fiji_national_rugby_league_team "Fiji national rugby league team") on the wing and scored a try in the 32–16 win at [Penrith Stadium](/wiki/Penrith_Stadium "Penrith Stadium").[[19]](#cite_note-19) On 18 July 2014, Vidot was demoted to the [Queensland Cup](/wiki/Queensland_Cup "Queensland Cup") along with [Jack Reed](/wiki/Jack_Reed_(rugby_league) "Jack Reed (rugby league)") and [Corey Oates](/wiki/Corey_Oates "Corey Oates") after they breached the club mid-week anti-alcohol policies, missing the Broncos match against the [New Zealand Warriors](/wiki/New_Zealand_Warriors "New Zealand Warriors") at [Suncorp Stadium](/wiki/Suncorp_Stadium "Suncorp Stadium").[[20]](#cite_note-20) Vidot late returned for the Broncos in Round 21 against the [Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles](/wiki/Manly-Warringah_Sea_Eagles "Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles") in the Broncos 16–4 loss at [Brookvale Oval](/wiki/Brookvale_Oval "Brookvale Oval").[[21]](#cite_note-21) In Round 24 against the [Newcastle Knights](/wiki/Newcastle_Knights "Newcastle Knights"), Vidot played his 100th NRL career match, scoring a try in the Broncos 48–6 win at Suncorp Stadium.[[22]](#cite_note-22)[[23]](#cite_note-23) Vidot finished off his first season with the Broncos with him playing in 23 matches and scoring 8 tries.

Vidot played in all of Samoa's 3 matches in the [2014 Four Nations](/wiki/2014_Rugby_League_Four_Nations "2014 Rugby League Four Nations") series, scoring 1 try.[[24]](#cite_note-24)[[25]](#cite_note-25)

### 2015

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daniel_Vidot&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: 2015")]

Vidot started the [2015 NRL season](/wiki/2015_NRL_season "2015 NRL season") in the [Queensland Cup](/wiki/Queensland_Cup "Queensland Cup") playing for the [Ipswich Jets](/wiki/Ipswich_Jets "Ipswich Jets") at [prop](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#prop_forward "Rugby league positions") when coach [Wayne Bennett](/wiki/Wayne_Bennett_(rugby_league) "Wayne Bennett (rugby league)") axed him due to his error riddled World Cup Series match against [Wigan](/wiki/Wigan_Warriors "Wigan Warriors").[[26]](#cite_note-26) On 25 March 2015, Vidot was granted compassionate leave from his Broncos contract after a deal with the [South Sydney Rabbitohs](/wiki/South_Sydney_Rabbitohs "South Sydney Rabbitohs") fell through.[[27]](#cite_note-27)[[28]](#cite_note-28) Vidot later earned his recall to Broncos NRL team in Round 5 against the [Gold Coast Titans](/wiki/Gold_Coast_Titans "Gold Coast Titans") in the Broncos 26–16 win at [Robina Stadium](/wiki/Robina_Stadium "Robina Stadium").[[29]](#cite_note-29)[[30]](#cite_note-30) On 2 May 2015, Vidot played for Samoa against [Tonga](/wiki/Tonga_national_rugby_league_team "Tonga national rugby league team") in the [2015 Polynesian Cup](/wiki/International_rugby_league_in_2015#Polynesian_Cup "International rugby league in 2015"), playing on the wing and scored a try in Samoa's 18–16 win at [Cbus Super Stadium](/wiki/Cbus_Super_Stadium "Cbus Super Stadium").[[31]](#cite_note-31)[[32]](#cite_note-32) Vidot finished the [2015 NRL season](/wiki/2015_NRL_season "2015 NRL season") with him playing in 8 matches and scoring 4 tries for the Broncos.

### 2016

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daniel_Vidot&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: 2016")]

On 11 November 2015, it was announced that Vidot had signed a 2-year contract with English [Super League](/wiki/Super_League "Super League") club, the [Salford Red Devils](/wiki/Salford_Red_Devils "Salford Red Devils"), starting in 2016.[[33]](#cite_note-33)[[34]](#cite_note-34)[[35]](#cite_note-35) Injuries prevented Vidot from making an immediate impact with the Red Devils, and he subsequently made his debut for Salford on 7 May in the [Challenge Cup](/wiki/Challenge_Cup "Challenge Cup") on the wing. However, he failed to score a try in the 32–18 loss.[[36]](#cite_note-36) Vidot scored his first two tries for the Red Devils in an 18–12 victory over the Widnes Vikings in Round 15 of the regular season.[[37]](#cite_note-37)

In addition to his move to the Super League, Vidot took part in a trial for [WWE](/wiki/WWE "WWE"), as he got the attention of wrestling talent scouts from the US because of his size, acting background, and perceived status as an elite athlete. He revealed in an interview that he initially received an offer for a trial from the company in 2015, but turned it down due to good form with the Broncos. He also stated he was a wrestling fan growing up, and would often imitate [The Undertaker](/wiki/The_Undertaker "The Undertaker").[[38]](#cite_note-38)[[39]](#cite_note-39)

Vidot ended his debut year in the Super League playing in 10 matches, and scoring 6 tries for Salford. At the end of the season, Vidot was released from his contract with Salford on compassionate grounds, citing a desire to return to Australia.[[40]](#cite_note-40)

### 2017

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daniel_Vidot&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: 2017")]

It was announced in late 2016 that the [Gold Coast Titans](/wiki/Gold_Coast_Titans "Gold Coast Titans") were interested in bringing Vidot back to the NRL. Vidot signed a one-year deal with the Titans, and was named in their [Auckland Nines](/wiki/Auckland_Nines "Auckland Nines") squad. Vidot played out the Titans 30–18 victory over the Parramatta Eels in the [Alice Springs](/wiki/Alice_Springs "Alice Springs") pre-season trial match, and made his club debut in Round 4, scoring a try in the 32–26 defeat against the North Queensland Cowboys. As the Titans favoured using Dale Copley and Anthony Don on the wings, Vidot was mainly relegated to the [Burleigh Bears](/wiki/Burleigh_Bears "Burleigh Bears"), the Titans' affiliate club in the Queensland Cup competition.[[41]](#cite_note-41) Vidot appeared for the Titans in three games in 2017, scoring two tries, before retiring from rugby league at the end of the season to pursue a professional wrestling career with WWE.

### 2024

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daniel_Vidot&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: 2024")]

On 5 Nov 2024 it was reported that he has been included in the [United States](/wiki/United_States_men%27s_national_rugby_league_team "United States men's national rugby league team") squad for the forthcoming tour of South Africa after turning out for [DC Cavalry](/wiki/Washington_DC_Cavalry "Washington DC Cavalry") in the [USARL](/wiki/USA_Rugby_League "USA Rugby League").[[42]](#cite_note-42)

### 2025

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daniel_Vidot&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: 2025")]

On 28 February 2025 he played for the [United States](/wiki/United_States_men%27s_national_rugby_league_team "United States men's national rugby league team") in a 46-10 loss to [Greece](/wiki/Greece_national_rugby_league_team "Greece national rugby league team").[[43]](#cite_note-43)

### 2026

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daniel_Vidot&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: 2026")]

On 27 February 2026 it was reported that he has been included in the [United States](/wiki/United_States_men%27s_national_rugby_league_team "United States men's national rugby league team") squad for the forthcoming fixture against [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland_national_rugby_league_team "Scotland national rugby league team") in Las Vegas.[[44]](#cite_note-44)

Vidot started at [second-row](/wiki/Rugby_league_positions#Second-row_forward "Rugby league positions"), where he scored a try in the 37th minute of the match, in a 28-20 victory for the USA over Scotland.[[45]](#cite_note-45)

## Professional wrestling career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daniel_Vidot&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Professional wrestling career")]

### WWE (2018–2024)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daniel_Vidot&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: WWE (2018–2024)")]

#### NXT (2018–2023)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daniel_Vidot&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: NXT (2018–2023)")]

In May 2018, Vidot signed a [developmental](/wiki/Farm_team "Farm team") contract with [WWE](/wiki/WWE "WWE").[[46]](#cite_note-46)

On 20 October 2020, Vidot's ring name **Xyon Quinn** was trademarked by WWE.[[47]](#cite_note-47) He debuted on *[NXT](/wiki/WWE_NXT "WWE NXT")* on 24 August 2021 in an upset victory over [Boa](/wiki/Boa_(wrestler) "Boa (wrestler)").[[48]](#cite_note-48) On 3 September, Quinn defeated Andre Chase in his *[205 Live](/wiki/WWE_205_Live "WWE 205 Live")* debut [[49]](#cite_note-205Live09032021-49)[[50]](#cite_note-205Live09242021-50)[[51]](#cite_note-NXT09282021-51)

On 9 November episode of *NXT*, he garnered the attention of Elektra Lopez and [Legado Del Fantasma](/wiki/Legado_Del_Fantasma "Legado Del Fantasma"), leading into a feud with the group's leader, [Santos Escobar](/wiki/Santos_Escobar "Santos Escobar").[[52]](#cite_note-NXT11092021-52)[[53]](#cite_note-NXT11162021-53) Quinn was defeated by Escobar on the 7 December edition of NXT, before the feud ended on 12 January, with Quinn once again failing to defeat Escobar.[[54]](#cite_note-NXT12072021-54)
On 10 May 2022 episode of *NXT*, Quinn turned [heel](/wiki/Heel_(professional_wrestling) "Heel (professional wrestling)") after confronting and attacking [Nathan Frazer](/wiki/Nathan_Frazer "Nathan Frazer").[[55]](#cite_note-NXT05102022-55) Quinn found little success as a heel, losing feuds to [Wes Lee](/wiki/Wes_Lee "Wes Lee") and Quincy Elliot.[[56]](#cite_note-NXT05312022-56) On 23 March 2023 episode of *NXT*, Quinn made his final appearance in NXT, competed in a 20-man battle royal to claim a spot in the fatal-five-way [NXT North American Championship](/wiki/NXT_North_American_Championship "NXT North American Championship") match at [Stand & Deliver](/wiki/NXT_Stand_%26_Deliver_(2023) "NXT Stand & Deliver (2023)"), being eliminated by [Oro Mensah](/wiki/Oro_Mensah "Oro Mensah").[[57]](#cite_note-57)

#### Main roster (2023–2024)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daniel_Vidot&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Main roster (2023–2024)")]

On 24 April 2020, Vidot made his on screen debut on an episode of *[SmackDown](/wiki/WWE_SmackDown "WWE SmackDown")* losing to [Sheamus](/wiki/Sheamus "Sheamus").[[58]](#cite_note-58) Quinn also wrestled a dark match in July 2021 on Smackdown.

In the [WWE Draft](/wiki/WWE_Draft "WWE Draft") in April 2023, Quinn was revealed as a free agent.[[59]](#cite_note-59) Quinn only made two appearances on the main roster in 2023 and 2024; competing in a 20-man Battle Royal to determine the number one contender for the [WWE Intercontinental Championship](/wiki/WWE_Intercontinental_Championship "WWE Intercontinental Championship") on an episode of Raw and losing to [Bron Breakker](/wiki/Bron_Breakker "Bron Breakker") in six seconds on Smackdown.[[60]](#cite_note-60)[[61]](#cite_note-61) On 19 April, Quinn was released from WWE, ending his 6 year stint with the promotion.[[62]](#cite_note-62)

### Total Nonstop Action Wrestling (2024)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daniel_Vidot&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Total Nonstop Action Wrestling (2024)")]

On 9 August 2024, Vidot made his debut in [Total Nonstop Action Wrestling](/wiki/Total_Nonstop_Action_Wrestling "Total Nonstop Action Wrestling") (TNA) under the ring name **Xyon**, facing [Steve Maclin](/wiki/Steve_Maclin "Steve Maclin") at *[Xplosion](/wiki/TNA_Xplosion "TNA Xplosion")* in a losing effort.[[2]](#cite_note-TNA-2)[[63]](#cite_note-63) He returned on 23 August episode of *Xplosion*, losing to [Sami Callihan](/wiki/Sami_Callihan "Sami Callihan").[[64]](#cite_note-64)

### All Japan Pro Wrestling (2025–present)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daniel_Vidot&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: All Japan Pro Wrestling (2025–present)")]

On 9 February 2025, Xyon was announced as one of the competitors for [All Japan Pro Wrestling](/wiki/All_Japan_Pro_Wrestling "All Japan Pro Wrestling")'s [Champion Carnival](/wiki/Champion_Carnival "Champion Carnival").[[65]](#cite_note-65) In August, he formed a stable, "HAVOC", with [Oddyssey](/wiki/Oddyssey_(wrestler) "Oddyssey (wrestler)") and [Shotaro Ashino](/wiki/Shotaro_Ashino "Shotaro Ashino").[[66]](#cite_note-HAVOC-66) On 23 September, at *Giant Dream 2025*, Xyon and Oddyssey defeated Baka No Jidai ([Hideki Suzuki](/wiki/Hideki_Suzuki "Hideki Suzuki") and [Kengo Mashimo](/wiki/Kengo_Mashimo "Kengo Mashimo")) to win the [World Tag Team Championship](/wiki/World_Tag_Team_Championship_(AJPW) "World Tag Team Championship (AJPW)"), making it the first title in Xyon's career.[[67]](#cite_note-GiantDream25-67)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Daniel_Vidot&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Personal life")]

In August 2018, Vidot proposed to fellow wrestler Danielle Glanville, better known as [Harley Cameron](/wiki/Harley_Cameron "Harley Cameron"), during their last night in Australia before migrating to America.[[68]](#cite_note-68) The couple split in 2024.[[69]](#cite_note-69)
